roles.title: Quality Assurance (QA) Engineer / Tester
roles.summary: Ensures software meets quality requirements by designing, executing, and automating tests throughout the development lifecycle.
responsibilities: Create and maintain test plans, test cases, and test data
Execute manual and automated functional, regression, and exploratory tests
Identify, document, and track bugs through resolution
Collaborate with developers and product managers on requirements
Integrate automated tests into CI/CD pipelines
Analyze test results and report on quality metrics
skills: Test design and clear bug reporting
Programming/scripting with Python, JavaScript, or Java
API and database testing including SQL
CI/CD and version control with Git
Analytical thinking and attention to detail

roadmap.title: Junior QA Engineer / Tester
roadmap.focus: Learn testing fundamentals, manual testing, and bug reporting.

roadmap.title: QA Engineer
roadmap.focus: Build automation frameworks, API tests, and CI integration.

roadmap.title: Senior QA Engineer
roadmap.focus: Design test architecture, mentor others, and broaden coverage.

roadmap.title: QA Lead / SDET
roadmap.focus: Define test strategy, build frameworks, and drive quality culture.

roadmap.title: QA Manager / Director of Quality
roadmap.focus: Lead quality organization, risk management, and metrics.

interactions.description: Pair on bug reproduction, clarify requirements, and review fixes.
Refine acceptance criteria and align test coverage with user outcomes.
Integrate tests into pipelines and monitor production quality signals.
Validate usability, accessibility, and design consistency.

bestPractices: Shift-left testing and early requirement review
Write clear, reproducible bug reports with steps and evidence
Automate regression suites and keep tests maintainable
Use risk-based prioritization for test coverage
Maintain traceability between requirements, tests, and defects

resources.title: Coursera QA Engineer Guide
resources.description: Overview of QA duties, skills, and career outlook.

resources.title: Ministry of Testing
resources.description: Community, courses, and resources for software testers.

resources.title: Playwright Documentation
resources.description: Modern cross-browser automation library and testing guide.

resources.title: Postman Learning Center
resources.description: API testing tutorials and best practices.

roles.title: Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE)
roles.summary: Embeds with customers to build production-grade solutions using the company's platform while feeding field insights back to product teams.
responsibilities: Embed with customers to understand workflows and requirements
Design and ship production code in client environments
Translate business problems into scalable platform solutions
Build data pipelines, integrations, and AI/LLM applications
Contribute reusable components and learnings to the core product
Collaborate with sales and customer success to expand adoption
skills: Full-stack and data engineering with Python, SQL, and JavaScript/TypeScript
Cloud and containerization with AWS/Azure/GCP, Docker, and Kubernetes
tools: Palantir Foundry / AIP or equivalent platforms
LLM APIs / vector databases / orchestration frameworks
Cloud consoles for AWS, Azure, and GCP

roadmap.title: Junior Forward Deployed Engineer
roadmap.focus: Learn platform internals and support customer implementations.

roadmap.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
roadmap.focus: Own customer use cases end-to-end and build pipelines and apps.

roadmap.title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer
roadmap.focus: Lead complex deployments, mentor others, and shape reusable patterns.

roadmap.title: Staff / Principal FDE
roadmap.focus: Drive platform strategy, architecture, and cross-customer playbooks.

roadmap.title: FDE Manager / Director
roadmap.focus: Scale the FDE organization, set delivery standards, and influence roadmap.

interactions.description: Co-locate or embed to discover pain points and validate solutions.
Channel field feedback into roadmap priorities and feature requirements.
Contribute code, reusable modules, and integration patterns.
Support expansion, proof-of-value, and successful onboarding.

bestPractices: Ship working production code, not just demos
Document reusable patterns and customer playbooks
Maintain tight feedback loops between field and product
Decompose ambiguous problems into scoped milestones
Prioritize customer outcomes and operational stability
